---
title: "Import PREDICTS Data and Filter 2001-2024"
author: "Eli Duggan"
date: "11/26/2025"
---

<!---
This file prepares PREDICTS site‑level data for downstream analysis.  It uses the `predictsr` package to download a consistent
release of the PREDICTS database, filters the data to a relevant date range, and retains only the columns needed for calculating
biodiversity indicator components and identifying sites.  The resulting cleaned table is saved as a CSV and used in subsequent steps.

**Summary of steps:**

1. Download the full PREDICTS database and cache via 'GetPredictsData()'.
2. Keep only abundance‑based measurements.
3. Convert the sampling midpoint to a Date and filter to 2000–2024 inclusive.
4. Select the columns needed to replicate merged_df_raw.csv (Biome, Predominant_land_use, Use_intensity, Kingdom–Genus, Diversity_metric, Diversity_metric_type, Effort_corrected_measurement, Reference, Site_name, Site_number, Latitude, Longitude, Sample_* and any other fields you want).
5. Save the filtered table as "a CSV"

-->

## The output files contain, respectively, the full filtered PREDICTS data, which can be used as inputs to the indicator calculation workflow.

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, message = TRUE, warning = TRUE)
```

## Install and load packages
```{r packages}
# install.packages("predictsr")

library(predictsr)
library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(lubridate)
```

## Configuration: extract years, date ranges, and output
```{r config}
# Extract complete PREDICTS dataset for 2016 and 2022 releases
extract_years <- c(2016, 2022)  

# Date range for filtering sample midpoint
date_min <- as.Date("2000-01-01")
date_max <- as.Date("2024-12-31")

# tmp and output directories
tmp_dir <- file.path("..", "tmp")
out_dir <- file.path("..", "output")
dir.create(tmp_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
```

## Download the full PREDICTS database with LoadPredictsData()
```{r download}
tmp_file <- file.path(tmp_dir, "predicts.rds")
predicts_raw <- LoadPredictsData(tmp_file, extract = extract_years)
```

## Filter to abundance metrics and 2000-2024 date range, select relevant columns 
```{r filter}
predicts_filtered <- predicts_raw %>%
  mutate(
    Sample_midpoint = as.Date(Sample_midpoint),
    Year            = year(Sample_midpoint)
  ) %>%
  filter(
    Diversity_metric_type == "Abundance",
    !is.na(Sample_midpoint),
    Sample_midpoint >= date_min,
    Sample_midpoint <= date_max
  )

cols_keep <- c(
  "Reference", "Study_number", "Site_name", "Site_number", "Source_ID", "SSBS",
  "Country",
  "Biome", "Predominant_land_use", "Use_intensity",
  "Years_since_fragmentation_or_conversion",
  "UN_subregion",
  "Hotspot",
  "Latitude", "Longitude",
  "Kingdom", "Phylum", "Class", "Order", "Family", "Genus",
  "Diversity_metric_unit",
  "Effort_corrected_measurement", "Measurement",
  "Sampling_effort", "Sampling_effort_unit", 
  "Sample_date_resolution", "Sample_start_earliest", "Sample_end_latest", "Sample_midpoint"
)

predicts_clean <- predicts_filtered %>% select(any_of(cols_keep))
```

## Output cleaned PREDICTS data as "predicts_filtered_2000_2024"
```{r write-out}
# Define output filename based on whether sampling was applied
output_file_name <- paste0(
  "predicts_filtered_",
  format(date_min, "%Y"), "_",
  format(date_max, "%Y"),
  ".csv"
)

write_csv(predicts_clean, out_dir, output_file_name)
```
